title: S1: disambiguate destinations that collide on label+path
action: Per S1 (Phase 2 of web-create-sheet), in web/src/views/create-sheet.ts's renderDestinations(), detect destinations that collide on {label, path} -- same label AND same path, including two destinations both having path: null -- with at least one other destination in the currently fetched list. For each COLLIDING destination only, append a short disambiguator to the displayed label: the last 4 characters of its own workspace_id, rendered as " · <suffix>" after the label -- the same "X · Y" caption pattern already used in switcher.ts's agent captions. A destination with no collision renders exactly as before: label alone, no suffix, byte-identical markup to what Phase 1 shipped. The suffix is purely a display concern -- the actual create requests (createPane/createAgent) must keep using dest.workspace_id in full, unabbreviated; do not let the suffix leak into any request body. Extract the collision-detection + suffix logic as a small pure function so it is unit-testable without rendering the DOM.

truths: Two or more destinations sharing both label and path (including all having path: null) each render with a · <suffix> appended to the label, using the last 4 characters of their own workspace_id
A destination with no matching label+path elsewhere in the current list renders with no suffix -- identical to Phase 1's output
The suffix never appears in a createPane or createAgent request body -- only the full dest.workspace_id is sent
Every pre-existing create-sheet.test.ts assertion continues to pass unmodified

artifacts.substantive: a small pure function computing the collision set and/or per-destination suffix, exported or otherwise directly unit-testable

key_links: renderDestinations() applies the suffix only to rows the collision function flags
prohibitions: Do not modify destinationCaveat(), handleAction(), or any create-request body construction
Do not touch web/src/views/switcher.ts, web/src/api.ts, or web/src/main.ts
Do not implement a backend-provided ordinal -- this stays pure client-side, derived only from workspace_id already in the payload (per S1)

verify: cd web && npm run typecheck && npm run test -- --run test/create-sheet.test.ts

trace.outcome: Added collisionSuffixes() pure function in create-sheet.ts; renderDestinations() now appends ' · <last-4-of-workspace_id>' to colliding {label,path} destination labels only, byte-identical markup otherwise. Added test coverage for collision incl. two null-path entries.
trace.verification_evidence: { "tests_inspected": ["web/test/create-sheet.test.ts"], "tests_added_or_changed": ["web/test/create-sheet.test.ts (new case: appends a workspace_id suffix only to destinations colliding on label+path, including two with a null path)"], "red_failure_evidence": "Pre-change create-sheet.ts (commit de1cb3d) rendered every destination label as `${escapeHtml(dest.label)}` with no suffix, unconditionally -- two destinations sharing {label, path} (e.g. both null-path 'no-folder' rows) were visually indistinguishable. The new test asserted against that pre-change source would fail: labels[1].textContent would be 'herdr-gateway' not 'herdr-gateway · 2222', and labels[3] would be 'no-folder' not 'no-folder · 4444'.", "verification_run": "cd web && npm run typecheck && npm run test -- --run test/create-sheet.test.ts -> tsc --noEmit clean; vitest 7 passed (7), including the 6 pre-existing unmodified assertions and the 1 new collision case", "deliberate_exceptions": [] }
trace.verify_output: tsc --noEmit: clean. vitest: create-sheet.test.ts (7 tests) 49ms, Test Files 1 passed (1), Tests 7 passed (7).
